context.description: Glob patterns for context files (`.view.yml`, `.topic.yml`, `.sql`, `.md`).  Resolved relative to the directory containing the YAML file.

databases.description:
Database names from config.yml to use as connectors.

Each string is the `name:` of a database entry in the project's `config.yml`.  The HTTP layer resolves these names to live connectors (via [`BuildContext`]) so that connection details are defined once and reused across agents.  All database types supported by Oxy (DuckDB, Postgres, BigQuery, Snowflake, ClickHouse, …) can be listed here.

```yaml databases: - local - analytics_db ```

instructions.description: Global instructions injected into every LLM call.

semantic_engine.description:
Optional vendor semantic engine configuration.

When absent, the solver routes exclusively through the internal semantic layer and LLM paths (identical to today's behaviour).

thinking.description:
Global thinking/reasoning config applied to all pipeline states.

Can be overridden per-state via the `states:` section.

```yaml thinking: adaptive       # shorthand # — or — thinking: budget_tokens: 10000   # explicit budget ```

validation.description:
Validation rule configuration.

When absent, all built-in rules run with their default parameters. Use this section to disable specific rules or tune parameters such as the outlier detection threshold:

```yaml validation: rules: solved: - name: outlier_detection enabled: true threshold_sigma: 3.0 min_rows: 6 ```

ExtendedThinkingConfigYaml.description:
Extended thinking mode preset configuration.

Overrides the default model and/or thinking config when the user activates "extended thinking" mode from the UI.

model.description: Model ID override for extended thinking mode.

thinking.description: Thinking config override for extended thinking mode.

api_key.description: API key.  Falls back to `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (Anthropic) or `OPENAI_API_KEY` (OpenAi / OpenAiCompat) environment variables.

base_url.description:
Base URL override.

- Anthropic: proxy URL (default: `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`) - OpenAi: Responses API base (default: `https://api.openai.com/v1/responses`) - OpenAiCompat: local server root, e.g. `http://localhost:11434/v1`

extended_thinking.description:
"Extended thinking" mode preset: an alternative model + thinking configuration activated at runtime via the UI toggle.

```yaml llm: thinking: effort:low extended_thinking: model: gpt-5.4 thinking: effort:medium ```

max_tokens.description: Maximum output tokens per call.  Defaults to 4096.

model.description: Model ID.  Overrides the `model_ref` resolved from `ref`.

ref.description:
Named model reference from the project's `config.yml`.

When set, the vendor, api_key, and base_url of that model entry are used as defaults and any other fields in this section override them.

```yaml llm: ref: openai-4o-mini   # model name from config.yml model: gpt-5.4        # override the model_ref only ```

thinking.description:
Default thinking/reasoning config for all pipeline states.

Takes precedence over the top-level `thinking:` field on `AgentConfig`.

```yaml llm: thinking: adaptive ```

vendor.description:
Backend vendor.  Defaults to [`LlmVendor::Anthropic`].

Overrides the vendor resolved from `ref` when both are set.

LlmVendor.description:
LLM vendor / backend selector.

```yaml llm: vendor: openai_compat   # Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, … model: llama3.2 base_url: http://localhost:11434/v1 ```

oneOf.description: OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`). Suitable for Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, and similar local/self-hosted backends.  Uses `OPENAI_API_KEY` as fallback (or pass `api_key` directly).

RuleEntry.description:
One entry in a rule list.

Extra TOML/YAML keys beyond `name` and `enabled` are collected into `params` via `#[serde(flatten)]`.  Each rule's constructor deserialises `params` into its own typed struct using [`serde_json::from_value`].

# Example YAML ```yaml - name: outlier_detection enabled: true threshold_sigma: 3.0 min_rows: 6 ```

SemanticEngineConfig.description:
Configuration for an external vendor semantic engine.

```yaml semantic_engine: vendor: cube base_url: https://cube.example.com api_token: "${CUBE_API_TOKEN}"

# — OR for Looker — semantic_engine: vendor: looker base_url: https://myco.looker.com client_id: "${LOOKER_CLIENT_ID}" client_secret: "${LOOKER_CLIENT_SECRET}" ```

instructions.description: Additional instructions injected for this state only.

model.description:
Model ID override for this state.

When set, this model is used instead of the global `llm.model`. The vendor, API key, and base URL from the global `llm:` section are inherited; only the model ID is replaced.

```yaml states: clarifying: model: claude-haiku-4-5   # cheap model for fast triage solving: model: claude-opus-4-6    # powerful model for SQL generation ```

ThinkingConfigYaml.description:
Thinking configuration as expressed in YAML.

Accepts either a shorthand string (`"adaptive"`, `"disabled"`) or a map with `budget_tokens`.

Because this enum is `#[serde(untagged)]`, schemars emits a JSON Schema `oneOf` with each variant represented by its structural shape (a plain string for [`ThinkingConfigYaml::Shorthand`] and an object for [`ThinkingConfigYaml::Manual`] / [`ThinkingConfigYaml::Effort`]). Serde discriminates at runtime by trying variants in declaration order, so there is no parse ambiguity. Some IDEs, however, validate each `oneOf` branch independently and may annotate a valid string with "invalid against variant 2 / variant 3" warnings — those are cosmetic and the file still deserializes correctly at runtime.

ValidationConfig.description: Validation section of the agent YAML config.

VendorKind.description:
Vendor identifier for bundled semantic engine adapters.

Internal to the config layer — never part of the `SemanticEngine` public API.
